title: Aglaomorpha drynarioides
taxonomy: Aglaomorpha drynarioides is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is commonly known as the drynaria tree fern. It belongs to the genus Aglaomorpha, which consists of about 6 species. The genus Aglaomorpha is part of the family Polypodiaceae, which includes about 2,000 species of ferns. Aglaomorpha drynarioides is native to tropical Asia and is often found in humid, shaded areas.

intro: Aglaomorpha drynarioides, also known as the Drynaria rhizome or Gu Sui Bu, is a type of epiphytic fern native to Asia, with its rhizome and root used in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain, swelling, and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Aglaomorpha drynarioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, such as aristolochic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare herbal supplements, teas, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to treat conditions like arthritis, gout, and eczema.
